evals.prompt: Migrate my BarcodeCount code from the old BarcodeCount.forDataCaptureContext constructor to the new SDK 7.6+ style. File: IntegratedApp.js
evals.expected_output: The skill reads migration.md and replaces BarcodeCount.forDataCaptureContext(context, settings) with new BarcodeCount(settings) followed by context.addMode(barcodeCount). All other existing code is preserved.

assertions.text: BarcodeCount.forDataCaptureContext( is not used in the migrated code (semantic)
new BarcodeCount(settings) is present in the output
context.addMode( is present and called with the barcodeCount instance
context.setMode( is NOT present (addMode is the correct call)
The BarcodeCount constructor does NOT receive context as an argument (semantic)
Existing BarcodeCountView setup and other code is preserved (semantic)
barcodeCount.isEnabled = true is still present
camera lifecycle code is preserved (semantic)

evals.prompt: I want to add not-in-list action support to my MatrixScan Count app. When users tap a barcode not on the packing list, they should see an action popup with Accept, Reject, and Cancel buttons. File: IntegratedApp.js
evals.expected_output: The skill reads migration.md and adds BarcodeCountNotInListActionSettings configuration on the BarcodeCountView with enabled=true, acceptButtonText, rejectButtonText, and cancelButtonText set. A BarcodeCountCaptureList is set on the barcodeCount. The view listener gains didTapAcceptedBarcode and didTapRejectedBarcode callbacks.

assertions.text: view.barcodeNotInListActionSettings is accessed
notInListSettings.enabled = true is set
acceptButtonText is set to a non-empty string
rejectButtonText is set to a non-empty string
cancelButtonText is set to a non-empty string
view.barcodeNotInListActionSettings = notInListSettings is reassigned after setting properties
barcodeCount.setBarcodeCountCaptureList( is called (required for not-in-list to have effect)
view.listener is set with didTapAcceptedBarcode and/or didTapRejectedBarcode callbacks
A comment or note mentions this feature requires SDK ≥7.1 on Capacitor (semantic)
Existing scan listener and camera code are preserved (semantic)

evals.prompt: Add the mapping flow to my MatrixScan Count Capacitor app. I want users to be able to see a spatial grid map of the scanned barcodes. File: IntegratedApp.js
evals.expected_output: The skill reads migration.md and updates the code to set settings.mappingEnabled = true, adds BarcodeCountMappingFlowSettings with customized text, and replaces the BarcodeCountView constructor with BarcodeCountView.forMapping(context, barcodeCount, style, mappingSettings). Includes a note that this is a beta API requiring SDK ≥8.3.

assertions.text: settings.mappingEnabled = true is set on BarcodeCountSettings
new BarcodeCountMappingFlowSettings() is instantiated
BarcodeCountView.forMapping( is called with context, barcodeCount, style, and mappingFlowSettings
The standard BarcodeCountView constructor (new BarcodeCountView({...}) or forContextWithMode) is replaced by forMapping (semantic)
connectToElement( is still called on the view
barcodeCount.isEnabled = true is still present
A comment or note mentions this is a beta API requiring SDK ≥8.3 (semantic)
Existing scan listener, camera, and context setup are preserved (semantic)
